ddls:
CREATE TABLE "trials" (
  "trial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trial */,
  "trial_number" INTEGER /* The number assigned to the trial */,
  "condition" TEXT /* The experimental condition or setup */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* The start time of the trial */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* The end time of the trial */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* The name of the experiment */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* The protocol followed during the trial */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the trial */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trial_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_identifier" TEXT /* Unique identifier given to the subject */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the subject */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the subject */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Medical history of the subject */,
  "medications" TEXT /* List of medications taken by the subject */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Subject's contact information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_position" TEXT /* The position of the sensor on the subject */,
  "channel" INTEGER /* The channel number of the sensor */,
  "name" TEXT /* The name of the sensor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the sensor */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "trial_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the trial during which the sample was collected */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subject on whom the data was collected */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that collected the data */,
  "sample_number" INTEGER /* The number of the sample within the trial */,
  "sensor_value" REAL /* The value recorded by the sensor */,
  "time" REAL /* The time at which the sample was recorded */,
  "synced_time" REAL /* Time synced with the trial start */,
  "quality_control" TEXT /* Quality control status of the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_trial_id FOREIGN KEY ("trial_id") REFERENCES trials ("trial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, lab technician, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "lab_affiliation" TEXT /* Lab affiliation of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "trial_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trial being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the trial */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the trial was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_reason" TEXT /* Reason for accessing the trial */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_trial_id FOREIGN KEY ("trial_id") REFERENCES trials ("trial_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "trial_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trial being versioned */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_trial_id FOREIGN KEY ("trial_id") REFERENCES trials ("trial_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permission_level" INTEGER /* Permission level of the role (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
